title: hemizonia congesta subsp. lutescens
intro: Hemizonia congesta subsp. lutescens, also known as Golden Tarweed, is a species of flowering plant in the sunflower family, native to the coastal mountains of California. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and pain, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. Golden Tarweed is often prepared as a tincture or infused oil, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
